session.user:
Update the following documentation files. The source code field `PlanRef.plan_id` has been renamed to `PlanRef.pr_id`. The `planned-pr-context/{plan_id}` branch pattern is now `planned-pr-context/{pr_number}` in source. JSON output field `"plan_number"` is now `"pr_number"`.
IMPORTANT: Many other source code identifiers still use `plan_id` (e.g., `state.plan_id`, `PlanContext.plan_id`, `resolve_plan_id_for_branch()`). Do NOT rename those references.
**File 1: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/ref-json-migration.md`** - `PlanRef` fields description: `plan_id (str)` → `pr_id (str)` - Migration table: `"plan_id"` field → `"pr_id"` - JSON example: `"plan_id": "7952"` → `"pr_id": "7952"` - Leave class name `PlanRef` unchanged
**File 2: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/plan-ref-architecture.md`** - All references to `PlanRef.plan_id` → `PlanRef.pr_id` - `plan_id` field descriptions for PlanRef → `pr_id` - Section heading "String `plan_id` Rationale" → "String `pr_id` Rationale" - Function signature `save_plan_ref(..., plan_id, ...)` → `save_plan_ref(..., pr_id, ...)` - `plan_id` String-to-Int section → `pr_id` - Code example: `plan_ref.plan_id.isdigit()` → `plan_ref.pr_id.isdigit()` - `"Non-numeric plan_id"` → `"Non-numeric pr_id"` - `numeric_plan_id` property → `numeric_pr_id` - Leave class names PlanRef, PlanNotFound unchanged - Leave mentions of `state.plan_id` unchanged if any
**File 3: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/issue-reference-flow.md`** - `plan_id` in the PlanRef context → `pr_id` - JSON example: `"plan_id": "123"` → `"pr_id": "123"`
Read each file first, then apply edits.
session.assistant: I'll help you update the documentation files to reflect the renaming from `plan_id` to `pr_id` for the `PlanRef` field. Let me start by reading each file to understand their current content.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: Ref JSON Migration 3→read_when: 4→ - "working with plan-ref.json or ref.json" 5→ - "understanding plan reference file formats" 6→ - "debugging plan reference loading failures" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "reading plan reference without using read_plan_ref()" 9→ warning: "Use read_plan_ref() which handles the three-file fallback chain: plan-ref.json → ref.json → issue.json (legacy). Manual JSON parsing skips fallback and field mapping." 10→ score: 5 11→--- 12→ 13→# Ref JSON Migration 14→ 15→Plan references have evolved through multiple file formats. The `read_plan_ref()` function handles all formats transparently via a fallback chain. 16→ 17→## PlanRef Dataclass 18→ 19→**Location:** `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_folder.py` 20→ 21→<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_folder.py, PlanRef --> 22→ 23→A frozen dataclass with fields: `provider` (PlanProviderType), `plan_id` (str), `url` (str), `created_at` (str, ISO 8601), `synced_at` (str, ISO 8601), `labels` (tuple of str), and `objective_id` (int or None). See `PlanRef` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_folder.py`. 24→ 25→## Required Fields Constant 26→ 27→<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_folder.py, _REQUIRED_REF_FIELDS --> 28→ 29→The private `_REQUIRED_REF_FIELDS` tuple in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_folder.py` defines which fields must be present. Used by `_parse_ref_json()` for LBYL validation before constructing `PlanRef`. See the source file for current fields. 30→
... [59 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: PlanRef Architecture 3→read_when: 4→ - "working with plan-ref.json" 5→ - "working with PlanRef dataclass" 6→ - "migrating from IssueReference to PlanRef" 7→ - "understanding provider-agnostic plan references" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "accessing plan_ref.plan_id as int without checking" 10→ warning: "plan_id is a string. Use LBYL: `plan_ref.plan_id.isdigit()` before `int(plan_ref.plan_id)`. Supports future non-numeric providers like 'PROJ-123'." 11→ - action: "calling save_plan_ref with positional arguments" 12→ warning: "All parameters after `impl_dir` are keyword-only. Positional calls will fail at runtime." 13→--- 14→ 15→# PlanRef Architecture 16→ 17→PlanRef is the provider-agnostic plan reference abstraction stored in `.erk/impl-context/plan-ref.json`. It replaced the GitHub-specific `IssueReference` to support future plan providers. 18→ 19→## Why PlanRef Replaced IssueReference 20→ 21→The original `IssueReference` was tightly coupled to GitHub: 22→ 23→- Fields: `issue_number` (int), `issue_url` (str) 24→- File: `.erk/impl-context/issue.json` 25→- Only supported GitHub issues as plan storage 26→ 27→PlanRef generalizes this: 28→ 29→- Fields: `provider`, `plan_id` (str), `url`, `labels`, `objective_id` 30→- File: `.erk/impl-context/plan-ref.json`
... [76 lines omitted] ...
50→Saves a plan reference to `plan-ref.json`. All parameters after `impl_dir` are keyword-only. Raises `FileNotFoundError` if `impl_dir` doesn't exist.

1→--- 2→title: Issue Reference Flow 3→read_when: 4→ - "issue references not appearing in PRs" 5→ - "debugging 'Closes #N' in PR body" 6→ - "working with plan-ref.json" 7→ - "closing reference lost after erk pr submit" 8→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:05 PT" 9→audit_result: clean 10→--- 11→ 12→# Issue Reference Flow 13→ 14→This document describes how issue references flow through the erk system, from creation to consumption in PR bodies. 15→ 16→## Creation 17→ 18→The `save_plan_ref()` function in `erk_shared/impl_folder.py` writes to `plan-ref.json`. 19→ 20→Parameters are keyword-only after `impl_dir`: 21→ 22→See `save_plan_ref()` in 23→[`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_folder.py`](../../../packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/impl_folder.py) 24→for the full signature. Key points: all parameters after `impl_dir` are keyword-only (`provider`, `plan_id`, `url`, `labels`, `objective_id`). 25→ 26→Called by: 27→ 28→- `setup-impl-from-pr` exec command - For local implementation 29→ 30→**Note:** `create_impl_context()` writes `ref.json` directly (not via `save_plan_ref()`). It uses a lighter format than `plan-ref.json`.
... [95 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant:
Perfect! I have successfully updated all three documentation files. Here's a summary of the changes made:
## Summary of Changes
### File 1: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/ref-json-migration.md` - Updated PlanRef dataclass description: `plan_id` → `pr_id` - Updated legacy field mapping table: `plan_id` → `pr_id` - Updated JSON schema example: `"plan_id": "7952"` → `"pr_id": "7952"`
### File 2: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/plan-ref-architecture.md` - Updated PlanRef fields list: `plan_id` → `pr_id` - Updated tripwire warnings: `plan_ref.plan_id` → `plan_ref.pr_id` - Updated section heading: "String `plan_id` Rationale" → "String `pr_id` Rationale" - Updated function signature: `save_plan_ref(..., plan_id, ...)` → `save_plan_ref(..., pr_id, ...)` - Updated field mapping comment: `issue_number` -> `pr_id` - Updated `validate_plan_linkage()` documentation: references to `plan_id` → `pr_id` - Updated section heading: "The `plan_id` String-to-Int Conversion Pattern" → "The `pr_id` String-to-Int Conversion Pattern" - Updated code example: `plan_ref.plan_id.isdigit()` → `plan_ref.pr_id.isdigit()` and `"Non-numeric plan_id"` → `"Non-numeric pr_id"`
### File 3: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/issue-reference-flow.md` - Updated function signature in save_plan_ref() parameters: `plan_id` → `pr_id` - Updated JSON data flow diagram: `"plan_id": "123"` → `"pr_id": "123"`
All updates specifically targeted `PlanRef.plan_id` and related field references while preserving other identifiers like `state.plan_id` and function names like `resolve_plan_id_for_branch()` as instructed.
